**Content Standards**

Blocking failure: the six implementation issues are still `type:task` leaves only. Per content standards, each `type:story`/`type:epic` must carry its own kebab-slug membership label. The approved plan’s “Decomposition (layered, dependency-ordered waves)” defines W1-W6 as wave/story-sized work, but the breakdown has no `type:story` layer at all, so there are no `story:*` labels for those buckets.

Concrete fix: convert the six wave issues into stories and add identifying story labels, then create child tasks below them:

```bash
jit issue update 04559558 --remove-label type:task --label type:story --label story:template-registry
jit issue update 643874f2 --remove-label type:task --label type:story --label story:apply-engine
jit issue update 3143ad87 --remove-label type:task --label type:story --label story:scaffold-switch
jit issue update 96fc8cd8 --remove-label type:task --label type:story --label story:planning-config-removal
jit issue update adc640e4 --remove-label type:task --label type:story --label story:skills-docs-migration
jit issue update 99a0c161 --remove-label type:task --label type:story --label story:template-testing-dogfood
```

Prior blocking content issue about unmarked criteria was addressed: the implementation issues now use `[hard]` criteria such as `REG-01`, `APPLY-01`, `SCAFFOLD-01`, etc. Titles are clean and do not contain ordinals or parent IDs.

**Coverage Vs Design**

| Design item | Issue | Matches? |
|---|---:|---|
| W1 — Template registry, `.jit/templates.toml` schema/loader, repo `plan` template, additive accessors | `04559558` | Yes |
| W2 — Apply engine, interpolation, anchor wiring, `move-upstream-to-role`, zero-node validation failure | `643874f2` | Yes |
| W3 — Add `jit apply`, switch scaffold atomically, breakdown consumes pre-created B, remove old commands | `3143ad87` | Yes |
| W4 — Migrate remaining `PlanningConfig` consumers, remove flat config and examples | `96fc8cd8` | Yes |
| W5 — Migrate skills, memory, docs, bracket id convention | `adc640e4` | Yes |
| W6 — Comprehensive tests, property tests, dogfood | `99a0c161` | Mostly |
| REQ-09 — Manual reseed existing empty planning-node descriptions, “not an engine deliverable” | `99a0c161` | No, mis-scoped |

Blocking failure: REQ-09 is still wrongly merged into `99a0c161`. The design says in “Design decisions”: “Backfill: manual (not engine) — reseed `1eb0bdfd`/`2937919f` by hand,” and W6 says “hand-reseed `1eb0bdfd`/`2937919f`.” The issue `99a0c161` is titled “Tests and dogfood for the graph-template engine” but contains `TEST-03: The existing empty planning-node descriptions are reseeded` and carries `satisfies:REQ-09`. That makes manual data cleanup part of the engine test/dogfood task.

Concrete fix:

```bash
jit issue create --type task --title "Reseed existing empty planning-node descriptions" --label epic:graph-templates --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-09
jit dep add <new-reseed-task> 96fc8cd8
jit issue update 99a0c161 --remove-label satisfies:REQ-09
```

Then remove this criterion from `99a0c161`:

```markdown
- [ ] [hard] TEST-03: The existing empty planning-node descriptions are reseeded.
```

If keeping three criteria, replace it with a true W6 test/dogfood criterion, for example:

```markdown
- [ ] [hard] TEST-03: Property tests verify apply results are acyclic, transitively reduced, gate-attached, force-idempotent, and based on the pre-apply dependency snapshot.
```

**Dependency DAG Correctness**

Blocking failure: the graph is still a single chain:

```text
99a0c161 -> adc640e4 -> 96fc8cd8 -> 3143ad87 -> 643874f2 -> 04559558 -> 59118ee9 -> 7d88e37d
```

The plan’s W6 dependency is “deps: W1-W4; W5 for skill-level dogfood.” The implemented edge `99a0c161 -> adc640e4` forces all engine tests, property tests, and manual reseeding to wait for skills/docs migration. That is an over-constraint and preserves the prior failed review’s dependency finding.

Concrete fix:

```bash
jit dep rm 99a0c161 adc640e4
jit dep add 99a0c161 96fc8cd8
```

If skill-level dogfood must wait for W5, split it out:

```bash
jit issue create --type task --title "Dogfood apply plan through migrated skills" --label epic:graph-templates --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-08
jit dep add <new-skill-dogfood-task> adc640e4
jit dep add <new-skill-dogfood-task> 99a0c161
```

Blank-workspace reachability: the only implementation root after the breakdown/planning bracket is `04559558`, and it can start on a fresh workspace because it creates the template registry and loader additively. No missing structural root edge found.

**Structural Integrity**

Blocking failure: the breakdown remains a flat set of six large `type:task` issues directly under the epic chain. The content standard requested here says large work should be multi-level, “epic -> story -> task,” and the approved plan explicitly calls these “layered, dependency-ordered waves.” Each current task contains multiple implementation units. Example: `3143ad87` bundles CLI addition, breakdown refactor, command removal, config migration, and test porting into one leaf.

Concrete fix: after converting W1-W6 to stories as shown above, add child tasks under each story matching the plan bullets. Minimum split for `3143ad87`:

```bash
jit issue create --type task --title "Add apply CLI dispatch" --label epic:graph-templates --label story:scaffold-switch --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-04
jit issue create --type task --title "Refactor breakdown to consume the pre-created breakdown node" --label epic:graph-templates --label story:scaffold-switch --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-07
jit issue create --type task --title "Remove old planning scaffold commands" --label epic:graph-templates --label story:scaffold-switch --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-04
jit issue create --type task --title "Port scaffold and breakdown tests" --label epic:graph-templates --label story:scaffold-switch --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-04
```

Repeat the same pattern for W1, W2, W4, W5, and W6 so the story issue describes the wave outcome and the child tasks describe implementable units.

**Prior Review Feedback**

Prior run `f33ce611` failed on four blocking findings. Only the unmarked success-criteria finding was addressed. The W6/REQ-09 mis-scope, the `99a0c161 -> adc640e4` over-constraint, and the flat task-only structure are all still present.

VERDICT: FAIL
